title: Task outcome: Read-only audit focused only on performance, scaling, SQLite sharding, index invalidation, and n
summary: Completed read-only performance/scaling audit of DinoBrain retrieval, wiki index, SQLite shards, invalidation paths, verifier scripts, and live data scale. No code files edited. Findings: no P0; P1 risks are that the live wiki SQLite fast path is missing/non-durable after invalidation, cold/noisy accepted memory is indexed as normal hot retrieval input, and candidate generation still uses substring/wildcard scans plus lexical dense fallback rather than bounded semantic top-K retrieval. Current final live snapshot had no wiki-index.json, no sqlite manifest, no wiki.sqlite, no dense-vectors.json; operations.sqlite and operations-index were present. Accepted records were dominated by behavior rules.

No code files edited. Findings: no P0; P1 risks are that the live wiki SQLite fast path is missing/non-durable after invalidation, cold/noisy accepted memory is indexed as normal hot retrieval input, and candidate generation still uses substring/wildcard scans plus lexical dense fallback rather than bounded semantic top-K retrieval. Current final live snapshot had no wiki-index.json, no sqlite manifest, no wiki.sqlite, no dense-vectors.json
request: Read-only audit focused only on performance, scaling, SQLite sharding, index invalidation, and noise control as DinoBrain data grows. Inspect wiki-index, sqlite-shards, retrieval, context, hybrid-retrieval, verify scripts, and current data scale. Return P0/P1 findings with evidence and fixes; do not edit files.
decisions: Treat current scalability as not complete while live wiki.sqlite, sqlite manifest, and dense-vectors.json are absent.
Recommend atomic or incremental wiki SQLite refresh instead of deleting the fast path and relying on manual rebuilds.
Recommend retrieval budgets and temperature/type-aware filtering so accepted behavior/task memories do not swamp Wiki/Source/Project knowledge.
Recommend FTS/prefix/vector top-K candidate generation and live performance canaries before claiming graph/LLM Wiki scale readiness.
next_steps: Implement durable SQLite wiki refresh/upsert and manifest freshness checks.
Add hot/warm/cold and memory-type retrieval budgets, with cold accepted memory requiring opt-in or second-pass retrieval.
Replace leading-wildcard and vocabulary-wide scans with FTS/prefix/trigram or bounded vector retrieval.
Add live-data and larger synthetic verifier thresholds for candidate counts, P95 latency, shard freshness, and fallback mode.

review_notes: Auto-held legacy unreviewed generated memory so it stays out of hot retrieval until lineage is manually confirmed.
